title: ligusticum aciphylla
intro: Ligusticum aciphylla, also known as mountain carrot or tarragon, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand's mountainous regions. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of ligustilide and other active compounds. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and providing antioxidant properties. Ligusticum aciphylla can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a versatile herbal remedy in traditional New Zealand medicine.
